title: How Codex and Anydocs Work Together
description: Put Codex inside an MCP-driven docs workflow.

render.markdown:
# How Codex and Anydocs Work Together

In Anydocs, Codex works best when it:

- reads the project contract first with `project_open`
- checks `project_open.authoring` for templates, resources, and metadata schemas
- reads `anydocs://authoring/guidance` when the task needs the full workflow
- inspects current page state before choosing create versus update
- uses `page_create_from_template`, `page_create`, or `page_update` for explicit changes
- treats `page_set_status` as a separate workflow action
- uses navigation MCP tools instead of rewriting navigation by hand

The main idea is not to have Codex edit `pages/*.json` directly, but to keep it on MCP tools.
render.plainText: Codex works best when it starts with project_open, checks authoring templates and metadata schemas, reads anydocs://authoring/guidance when needed, and uses page and nav MCP tools for explicit changes. The goal is to avoid direct edits to pages JSON and navigation JSON.
